book_claims.claim: Celery is used in the Longevity Diet tomato soup base, with 1 medium stalk per recipe.
book_claims.mechanism: Aromatic base vegetable providing 3-n-butylphthalide (3nB), apigenin, and luteolin — bioactives with anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular properties. Also contributes flavor complexity that supports adherence to the plant-forward diet.

supplementary_research.source: Sowbhagya, Critical Reviews in Food Science and Nutrition (2014) — PMID 24279396
supplementary_research.finding: Celery's 3-n-butylphthalide (3nB) is a potent antihypertensive compound shown to lower blood pressure by relaxing smooth muscle in arterial walls; studies show ~14% reduction in systolic BP with celery seed extract, supporting cardiovascular longevity.

Apigenin and luteolin from celery demonstrate significant neuroprotective effects in in vitro and animal models by inhibiting neuroinflammation through NF-kB suppression and AMPK activation, mechanisms relevant to Alzheimer's disease prevention and cognitive aging.

supplementary_research.source: Tang et al., PLOS ONE (2017) — PMID 28700697
supplementary_research.finding: Celery seed extract reduced serum uric acid levels and alleviated gout-related inflammation in animal models; celery consumption has traditional use in joint health, relevant to maintaining mobility and quality of life in aging.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Lipophilic; absorption enhanced by fat; primary antihypertensive compound in celery; concentrated in seeds (~2-3x stalk levels).
Flavonoid with low but meaningful oral bioavailability; gut microbiota convert apigenin-glycosides to free apigenin; anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective.
Fat-soluble; best absorbed with dietary fat; important for coagulation and bone matrix protein activation.
Well absorbed; supports blood pressure regulation through sodium counterbalance.

synergies.description: In the Longevity Diet soup, celery's 3nB (antihypertensive) and tomato's lycopene (cardiovascular antioxidant) create a complementary cardiovascular-protective combination.
Celery and carrots form the classic mirepoix pairing; together they provide a broader range of carotenoids, flavonoids, and phthalides than either alone.
Olive oil enhances absorption of celery's fat-soluble bioactives (3nB, vitamin K) and contributes anti-inflammatory oleocanthal to the dish.
